texts.content:
Notice to All Tenants
Subject: Building Maintenance — Elevator Outage

Please be advised that the passenger elevators in Tower B will be out of service from 8:00 PM on Friday, September 12, until 6:00 AM on Monday, September 15, for scheduled maintenance. Freight elevators will remain operational during this period.

We apologize for any inconvenience. Residents on floors 8 and above who require assistance with heavy items may contact the front desk to schedule a time slot for freight elevator use.

Management recommends using the staircase for floors 1 through 7 during the outage.

questions.stem: What is the primary reason for this announcement?
options: A. To warn tenants about a building emergency
B. To inform tenants about planned elevator maintenance
C. To announce the installation of new elevators
D. To notify tenants about a change in building management
questions.explanation: The announcement is about 'scheduled maintenance' of the passenger elevators, informing tenants in advance.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the freight elevators during the maintenance period?
options: A. They will be available for all tenants to use freely
B. They will be exclusively for maintenance workers
C. They can be used by arrangement for moving heavy items
D. They will also be undergoing maintenance at the same time
questions.explanation: The announcement says residents can 'contact the front desk to schedule a time slot for freight elevator use,' implying it is available by appointment for specific needs.

texts.content:
Monthly Budget Planner — Step 1: Track Your Income

Before you can build a budget that works, you need to know exactly how much money is coming in each month. Start by listing all sources of income:

• Primary job salary (after taxes)
• Freelance or side business earnings
• Investment dividends
• Government benefits or student grants

Pro Tip: Use your net income (after-tax amount), not your gross income. This gives you a realistic picture of what you actually have available to spend and save.

Next week: Step 2 — Categorize Your Expenses.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of this webpage?
options: A. How to reduce monthly expenses
B. How to invest money for retirement
C. The first step in creating a monthly budget
D. How to apply for government benefits
questions.explanation: The webpage is titled 'Monthly Budget Planner — Step 1' and focuses on tracking income as the first step in budgeting.

questions.stem: The word 'net' in 'net income' most likely refers to:
options: A. the total earnings before any deductions
B. the amount remaining after taxes and deductions
C. the estimated income for the next quarter
D. the combined income of all household members
questions.explanation: The webpage defines net income as 'after-tax amount,' meaning what remains after taxes and deductions.

questions.stem: What does the webpage imply about using gross income for budgeting?
options: A. It is the preferred method for most experts
B. It leads to an unrealistic spending plan
C. It works better for freelance workers
D. It is required for government grant applications
questions.explanation: The webpage advises using net income because it 'gives you a realistic picture,' implying that gross income would overstate what is actually available.
